subject: This week's 🦄 AI That Works session was on 'AI Content Pipeline Revisited: Automating Our Podcast Production'!
body:
Hello First Name,

Hope you caught our latest 🦄 AI That Works session! We dove deep into "AI Content Pipeline Revisited: Automating Our Podcast Production."

If you missed it, no worries! The full recording, code, and diagrams are now up on GitHub:
https://github.com/hellovai/ai-that-works

We covered a ton about automating the entire production pipeline for the AI That Works podcast. Here’s a quick rundown of what we discussed:

*   **It's All About Smart Orchestration:** We showed how Claude Code can act as the central brain, bringing together different tools like BAML, NanoBanana, and even browser agents to automate everything from generating images to scheduling events.
*   **Humans Still Rule (The Loop):** Automation isn't about going completely hands-off. We really stressed how crucial human review and intervention are for important outputs (like emails and public posts) and for refining AI-generated content.
*   **Making AI Content Sound Human:** We shared our approach to generating freeform text that sounds natural and authentic. It's all about using structured outputs, identifying patterns, and making targeted fixes to get the tone just right.

So, what's the main idea we want you to remember from this session?
Effective AI content automation isn't about 100% hands-off. It's about strategically integrating human-in-the-loop processes, using AI agents to orchestrate tasks, and constant feedback to ensure everything you put out is high-quality, controlled, and sounds genuinely *you*.

Speaking of which, our next session tomorrow is another 'No Vibes Allowed' live coding event! We'll be diving into advanced context engineering principles to ship some real features in Riptide.Write.
Sign up here: https://lu.ma/zcf5c8yd

Got questions? Just hit reply to this email or jump into our Discord: https://www.boundaryml.com/discord. We're always happy to chat! Happy coding 🧑‍💻

Vaibhav & Dex
call_to_action: Sign up for tomorrow's 'No Vibes Allowed' live coding event.
